name: Cccs Of Lss - South Dakota
description_short: LSS of South Dakota is a nonprofit offering free and low-cost financial counseling and mental health services to individuals and families across South Dakota.
description_long:
LSS of South Dakota is a faith-inspired nonprofit organization dedicated to strengthening individuals, families, and communities throughout South Dakota. Founded on the mission that all people in South Dakota will be healthy, safe, and accepted, LSS operates a comprehensive continuum of social services rather than exclusively focusing on consumer finance. The organization serves people of all ages, faiths, races, and economic backgrounds.

Within their broader service portfolio, LSS provides Financial Counseling & Education designed to help people achieve financial stability, along with Behavioral Health Services offering flexible and affordable mental health counseling. These services are part of their Mental & Financial Wellness division. The organization positions itself as offering a whole-person approach to care, meaning financial guidance is integrated with other support services including family stabilization, youth services, refugee support, and older adult programs. This integrated model allows clients to receive tailored support based on their complete circumstances.

What distinguishes LSS is its scope as a full-service social services nonprofit rather than a dedicated credit counseling agency. They operate residential facilities, mentorship programs, foster care services, and community centers alongside financial services. The organization is responsive to regional needs and actively evolves its offerings. They actively recruit volunteers and staff, indicating ongoing expansion and community investment.

The main caveat is that while financial counseling is offered, LSS is primarily a comprehensive social services organization. The website provides limited specific details about their financial counseling program structure, certification status, fees, or service delivery methods. Consumers seeking dedicated credit counseling from HUD-approved or NFCC-certified counselors may need to contact LSS directly to confirm credentials and program specifics.
pros: Nonprofit organization with no profit motive, aligned with client financial wellness
Integrated approach combines financial counseling with mental health, family support, and other services
Serves diverse populations including refugees, immigrants, youth, families, and older adults
Offers free or low-cost services across South Dakota to people of all economic backgrounds
Active volunteer program indicates strong community engagement and accessibility
Part of larger continuum means clients can access multiple support services through one organization
Transparent mission and values emphasizing compassion, respect, and ethical service
cons: Website provides minimal details about financial counseling program structure, fees, or eligibility requirements
No clear indication of HUD-approval or NFCC certification status for financial counseling services
Limited specific information about counseling delivery methods (in-person, phone, online availability)
As a broad social services agency, financial counseling may not be their primary focus or specialty
Website content does not specify debt counseling, credit repair guidance, or specialized financial services
best_for: South Dakota residents seeking free or low-cost financial counseling combined with other social services
Families needing integrated support including financial guidance, mental health services, and family stabilization
Vulnerable populations including refugees, immigrants, youth, and older adults with financial stability needs
Individuals looking for faith-based nonprofit financial support aligned with values-driven mission
diagnosis: LSS of South Dakota is best for South Dakota residents seeking free or low-cost financial counseling integrated with broader social services and mental health support, particularly those facing multiple life challenges. The main caveat is that while they offer financial counseling, they are primarily a comprehensive nonprofit social services organization rather than a specialized credit counseling agency; prospective clients should contact them directly to confirm program credentials, specific services offered, and delivery methods.
services: Financial Counseling & Education for achieving financial stability
Behavioral Health Services with flexible and affordable mental health counseling
Family Stabilization Services supporting goal-setting and resource connection
Refugee & Immigration Support including workforce training
Older Adult Services for independent living
Youth Residential Services with psychiatric treatment
Multi-Cultural Center with interpreting and community engagement
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
